Center: IAEA Nuclear Data Section, Vienna, Austria
title: Elastic scattering of 11.8-MeV deuterons from several elements

x4freetext: Elastic scattering of 11.8-MeV deuterons from
several elements
Natural targets
The scattered particles pass out of the
scattering chamber through thin plastic windows and
pass into a detector consisting of three proportional
counters and CsI(Tl) crystal.
In addition, a CsI(Tl) monitor counter was used. The
Faraday cup intercepts over 99% of the beam passing
through the target (except for Rh and Ta).
The error in the absolute cross-sections
is caused mostly by the inhomogeneity of the target
foils.
The error in the relative cross section is
+-3% between 30 and 120 degr. and, mostly due to the
statistical uncertainty in the number of counts, up to
+-5% at backward angles. From 30 to 20 degr. the error
increases to +-10% due to the uncertainty in the of
the measurement of the small currents involved.
Digitizing error
Digitizing error
SD
